verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1/TMP21) is a type I transmembrane protein of the p24 family that resides predominantly in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and Golgi apparatus, cycling between these compartments via COPI/COPII vesicles. Its primary function is in cargo selection and vesicle budding within the early secretory pathway. Although it has a single-pass TM domain and a luminal/extracellular domain, its steady-state localization is endomembrane, not the plasma membrane. No documented stable PM pool or surface-accessible form is established for TMED10.
